title: $35 MILLION Beat Thomas Massie — Most Expensive House Primary EVER
title_alt: Thomas Massie LOST. Here's The $35M FEC Receipt.
$16M In Outside Ads Removed One "No" Vote — Massie's Story
Massie Says 95% Of His Opponent's Money Came From The Israeli Lobby
description:
Thirty-five million dollars in one House primary. The most expensive primary for a single seat in American history. Thomas Massie is out, and the receipts are public.

Kentucky's Fourth district sits across the river from Cincinnati. Massie — a libertarian Republican who voted no on Trump's tax & immigration package, voted no on foreign-aid bills including Israel funding, and forced votes to release the Epstein files — became the most expensive primary loss in U.S. House history. Roughly $16M in outside ads ran against him. Three groups did the heavy lifting: UDP (the AIPAC super-PAC), the Republican Jewish Coalition, and Miriam Adelson's Preserve America PAC. Days before the primary, Massie himself put a number on it on CBS News: "95% of my opponent's money comes from the Israeli lobby." Trump endorsed Ed Gallrein. Gallrein won by about ten thousand votes.

📋 Full receipt: https://campaignreceipts.com/race/ky-04-2026-r-primary

⏱ Chapters
0:00 $35M in one House primary (most expensive ever)

3:33 Election night — Gallrein wins, Massie concedes
3:54 The verdict: $35M came to remove one "no" vote

📚 Sources
• Federal Election Commission — Schedule E independent expenditures · United Democracy Project, Republican Jewish Coalition, Preserve America PAC (committee C00878801) · KY-4 cycle 2025–26
• Kentucky Secretary of State — certified Republican primary results, May 19, 2026
• CBS News — pre-primary interview with Rep. Thomas Massie (fair-use ≤30s)
• LiveNOW from FOX — Ed Gallrein victory remarks (fair-use ≤30s)

Campaign Receipts is a nonpartisan campaign-finance channel. Same skepticism for Israel-lobby spending against an R libertarian as for the same lobby's spending against D progressives like Cori Bush. Receipts. Subscribe for more.
